This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier."},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

returns._price: The current price of the pool in token1.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

upgradeTo(address).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

beefyFeeConfig().notice: The address of the beefy fee config, set on the factory.

beefyFeeRecipient().notice: The address of the beefy fee recipient, set on the factory.

depositFee().notice: The deposit fee variable will alwasy be 0. This is used by the UI.

factory().notice: The address of the strategy factory

getAllFees().notice: get all the fees from the fee config for this contract

getStratFeeId().notice: get the strat fee id from the fee config

keeper().notice: The address of the keeper, set on the factory.

lastHarvest().notice: The last time the strat harvested

lastPositionAdjustment().notice: The last time we adjusted the position

lockedProfit().notice: The locked profit is the amount of token0 and token1 that is locked in the vault, this can be overriden by the strategy contract.

moveTicks().notice: Rebalance the position. Gated by onlyOwner (owner = vault) so rebalance         is routed through vault.rebalanceAMM(), giving the vault control over         when its strategy repositions.

native().notice: The native address of the chain

price().notice: The current price of the pool in token1, encoded with 36 decimals.

setStratFeeId(uint256).notice: set the strat fee id in the fee config

totalLocked0().notice: The total amount of token0 locked in the vault

totalLocked1().notice: The total amount of token1 locked in the vault

withdrawFee().notice: The withdraw fee variable will alwasy be 0. This is used by the UI.
